examples.title: Friend Function Declaration and Access
examples.description: Basic example showing how to declare and use a friend function to access private data
examples.explanation: The friend function displayLength can access the private 'length' member directly using the object b. It is called like a normal function, not using dot operator.

examples.title: Operator Overloading with Friend Function
examples.description: Overloading + operator using friend function for a complex number class
examples.explanation: The operator+ is declared as a friend to allow natural syntax (c1 + c2) rather than c1.operator+(c2). Both operands are explicitly passed.

examples.title: Stream Operator Overloading with Friend
examples.description: Overloading << operator to display class objects using cout
examples.explanation: Returning ostream& allows chaining: cout << p1 << p2. Without friend, we'd have to write p1 << cout which is unnatural.

examples.title: Friend Class Example
examples.description: Demonstrates a friend class accessing private members of another class
examples.explanation: Car class is declared as friend of Engine, so all Car member functions can access Engine's private horsepower and type members directly.

examples.title: Demonstrating Friendship Properties
examples.description: Shows that friendship is not transitive and not inherited
examples.explanation: This demonstrates that friendship is NOT inherited. Even though C inherits from B (which is a friend of A), C cannot access A's private members.
